channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Florida hotel discriminated against Arab American group after Hamas' Oct. 7 attack against Israel, DOJ says
item.description: A Florida hotel owner reached an agreement with the DOJ, which accused the hotel of discrimination after it canceled a planned conference for an Arab American group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Free upgrades and pardon requests: What it's like to share a name with a president
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There’s not only one Donald Trump in the United States. Nor is there only one William J. Clinton. Plenty of people share names with famous presidents. Donald Trump, a veteran and resident of Alabama, said because of his name he often is gifted things for free. William J.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters from the Navajo Nation worked tirelessly through a haze of dust to cut away dirt from a narrow road at the side of a mountain struck by a landslide in Southern California, coughing and sneezing amid the backbreaking work.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LOCHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump will arrive in Washington Saturday for inaugural celebrations to mark his return to power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump will return to Washington Saturday to kick off days of pageantry to herald his second inauguration as president, four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Resettlement agencies race to help refugees ahead of Trump's second term
item.description: Resettlement agencies have raced to welcome refugees before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump vowed to end the war in Ukraine quickly but Moscow and Kyiv are digging in before any talks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has pledged to broker a peace deal in Ukraine, but as he prepares to take office, peace seems as elusive as ever. Moscow and Kyiv are seeking battlefield gains to strengthen their negotiating positions ahead of any prospective talks to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's first trip as president will be to Los Angeles: Sources
item.description: The first official trip of Trump's second administration will be to visit areas devastated by fires in California.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — More than 40 Pakistanis were feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a boat off West Africa's Atlantic coastline, which has emerged as a primary point of departure for migrants aiming to reach Europe.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man charged after dressing to 'appear as an Orthodox Jewish rabbi,' entering Jewish community center
item.description: An East Tennessee man faces multiple charges after allegedly entering a Jewish community center dressed to to "appear as an orthodox Jewish Rabbi," according to the Metro Nashville Police Department (MNPD).
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey teacher charged with allegedly sexually assaulting former student, having his child
item.description: A New Jersey teacher is facing charges after police say she is accused of sexually abusing one of her students and having his child.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Cristelli, NewsNation

item.title: Smoke from fire at California lithium battery plant raises concerns about air quality
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fire at the world’s largest battery storage plant in Northern California smoldered Friday after sending plumes of toxic smoke into the atmosphere, leading to the evacuation of up to 1,500 people. The blaze also shook up the young battery storage industry.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Israel approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: First responders saved a moose that fell through lake ice in New York. Here's how they did it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bull moose that fell through lake ice in the Adirondack Mountains was saved by New York conservation officials in a laborious cold-water rescue. The moose fell through the ice around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden announced Friday that he was commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses, using his final days in office on a flurry of clemency actions meant to nullify prison terms he deemed too harsh.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A battery plant fire in California started during a boom for energy storage
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A fire at one of the world’s largest battery plants in Northern California contained tens of thousands of lithium batteries that store power from renewable energy and have become a growing electricity source.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This fast-food restaurant has the ‘best’ chicken nuggets, new report says
item.description: (KTLA) — Chick-fil-A, often the go-to place for many to get chicken sandwiches, is also the best place to get chicken nuggets, according to the Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Fires scorched campuses across Los Angeles. Many schools are seeking places to hold classes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Days after losing her home in the same fire that destroyed her Los Angeles elementary school, third-grader Gabriela Chevez-Muñoz resumed classes this week at another campus temporarily hosting children from her school.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, JOCELYN GECKER and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honoring Trump: Red state governor to fly MAGA flag on Inauguration Day
item.description: Louisiana Governor Jeff Landry announced that the President-elect Trump's famed Make America Great Again flag will fly at the Governor’s Mansion on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to save your TikTok videos, data ahead of looming ban
item.description: Barring a last-minute turnaround, the popular social media app will be unavailable in all 50 states Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Joan Plowright, Tony Award-winning British actor and widow of Laurence Olivier, dies at 95
item.description: Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, who with her late husband Laurence Olivier did much to revitalize the U.K.'s theatrical scene in the decades after World War II, has died. She was 95.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley Act, teeing up final vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday to advance the Laken Riley Act, putting Republicans a step closer to sending the first bill of the 119th Congress to President-elect Trump for his signature next week and giving him an early win on one of his key issues.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president. He did that for George H.W. Bush, his successor and vice president of eight years.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Water systems expert has a message for the public as LA wildfires rage
item.description: Expert Kathryn Sorensen discusses issues with municipal water systems and why she believes that the Santa Ynez Reservoir might not have made a massive difference had it been full.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top music acts announced for FireAid, a concert to support L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: The biggest names in pop, rock and country are coming together to raise funds for victims of the Palisades and Eaton wildfires and assist with the recovery.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok says it will 'go dark' unless it gets clarity from Biden following Supreme Court ruling
item.description: TikTok says it will have to “go dark” this weekend unless the outgoing Biden administration assures the company it won’t enforce a shutdown of the popular app after the Supreme Court unanimously upheld the federal law banning the app unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Mel Gibson ‘just as surprised’ as anyone to be named Trump ‘special envoy’ to Hollywood
item.description: Actor and film producer Mel Gibson said he was stunned to be appointed a special envoy to Hollywood under the upcoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration picks 15 more drugs for Medicare negotiation
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) has selected 15 more drugs for Medicare price negotiation, announcing the selection two weeks ahead of schedule.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Massachusetts migrants taking shelter beds from taxpayers, allowed in with no vetting: former director
item.description: Massachusetts' overcrowded shelter system has allowed immigrants to enter without vetting and keep taxpayers out in the cold, a former director says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orlando drone show crash caused by 'combined errors' that led to misaligned flight path: NTSB report
item.description: A preliminary report into what went wrong at a Florida drone show in December, causing some of the aircraft to go rogue, has been released by the National Transportation Safety Board.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mud, debris slide splits hillside home that survived Palisades Fire in half
item.description: A hillside home overlooking the Pacific Ocean that survived the devastating Palisades Fire has split in half from a landslide directly behind the property.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, ABC 4

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, ABC 4

item.title: Biden warns of dangers of 'oligarchy' of ultra-rich, 'tech-industrial complex' running country
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
